Tikur~Anbessa~Specialized~Hospital~(TASH: The College of Health Sciences (CHS) is a professional health sciences college, established in 2009/10 by the reorganization of previously separate institutions of health under one umbrella. The CHS is comprised of four schools and one teaching hospital. The four schools are the School of Medicine (SoM), the School of Pharmacy (SoP), the School of Public Health (SPH) and the School of Allied Health Sciences (SAHS). The SAHS offers professional training in nursing, midwifery and medical laboratory technology. The rationale behind the establishment of the CHS is to encourage cross-breeding across various disciplinary paradigms;
AAU~Museums: The Ethnographic Museum of the Institute of Ethiopian Studies which is located at the main campus of Addis Ababa University (AAU) is the first university museum in Ethiopia. Since 1963 it has been serving the public at large in awareness creation of the about Ethiopia's diverge population of cultural resources. It is cultural institution dedicated to the preservation, reserve and presentation of Ethiopia's rich cultural heritage. The museum was established with the intention of conserving the nation's heritage, conducting pertinent research, and supporting educational endeavors of the People of Ethiopia Its collection exceeds 16,000 items, including religious and secular manuscripts, ceremonial items, icons, paintings, basketry, poetry, hand crafts, traditional weapons and musical instruments textiles, as well as, crosses, and philatelies. Some artifacts even date back to the early Aksumite period. The Ethnographic Museum is also a place where visitors enjoy as well as researchers conduct their research actively in projects related to heritage and museum studies. It is open to visitors from Monday to Sunday, excluding public holidays. Guides are available at all Times to provide information and insights about the collections. Entrance fees are charged at different rates for adults and students.
Book~Center: The University Book Center is the official bookshop for the entire Addis Ababa University. The Center boasts a large selection of textbooks, reference materials, monographs, journals. Also available are general-interest books, literature, technical books, and best sellers for customers from within and outside of AAU. The range of books, many of them published by the AAU Press, covers virtually all fields by Ethiopian and foreign authors. The book center also sells a broad array of other educational and office stationery supplies, and related items.
AAU~Startups: The Addis Ababa University Startups Center is a dynamic hub for innovation and entrepreneurship, dedicated to empowering students, researchers, and early-stage entrepreneurs as they develop transformative ideas into scalable businesses. In addition to its main facilities, the center includes specialized innovation branches across other AAU campuses, such as the AAiT Innovation Center, the Science Innovation Center, and the Black Lion Hospital Health Innovation Center. These centers provide tailored resources, mentorship, and networking, opportunities to support development across diverse fields-from: technology and engineering to health sciences- driving Ethiopia's economic growth and addressing real world challenges through sustainable Innovation.
Sports~and~Recreation: AAU is dedicated to the provision of recreational and competitive sports for students, staff and faculty. A broad range of sporting events basketball, handball, tennis, athletics, table tennis, chess involve mass participation through the fan activities. Sports include inter-years, and inter-faculty sports competitions.
